quick_facts.label: Role classification
quick_facts.value: In a recent central-government recruitment rule, Upper Division Clerk is classified as a General Central Service, Group ‘C’, non-gazetted, ministerial post.

quick_facts.label: Common promotion route
quick_facts.value: UDC is commonly a promotion grade for LDC/JSA staff, but the qualifying service, tests, and selection method are governed by the employing organisation’s recruitment rules. For example, Puducherry’s 2023 LDCE notice required eligible Level-2 LDCs to have four years’ regular service and pass specified departmental tests; another central recruitment rule provides promotion from Level-2 LDC after five years’ regular service on seniority-cum-fitness.

quick_facts.label: Typical pay-matrix context
quick_facts.value: Many central-government UDC posts are placed at 7th CPC Pay Level 4 (₹25,500–81,100 basic-pay matrix), although the applicable level and total take-home remuneration must be checked in the employer’s current rules or vacancy notice.

quick_facts.label: Core work
quick_facts.value: Official duty statements describe UDC work as section-level administrative and ministerial work, including correspondence, records and files, service/leave records, returns, bills or accounts, and—in some offices—cash or stores work.

quick_facts.label: Possible onward progression
quick_facts.value: Progression is employer- and cadre-specific. Examples include promotion from UDC to Office Superintendent in the Office of the Development Commissioner (MSME), while ISRO lists a UDC-to-Assistant career prospect subject to vacancy and satisfactory performance.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
paragraphs: UDC is a clerical-administrative role whose exact portfolio depends on the section and department. A Government of India technical institute’s duty schedule assigns UDCs administrative and ministerial work; office correspondence; maintenance of personal files, leave accounts and service books; returns; cash and accounts work; bill preparation; GPF-account work; storekeeping; and maintenance of office records, files and accounts.

A separate central-government office describes UDC duties more compactly as cash work, typing and miscellaneous work across sections. Taken together, these official descriptions support viewing UDC as a records-, correspondence- and process-oriented post rather than a single uniform job description across all governments and cadres.
bullets: Maintain and process files, registers, service records and related office documentation.
Draft, type, diarise or process correspondence and routine section communications where assigned.
Handle establishment, leave, bills, cash, accounts, returns or stores functions when posted to those sections.
Carry out other section work assigned under the office’s local allocation of duties.

sections.heading: Entry routes
paragraphs: For the career path represented by this node, the principal route is internal advancement from LDC/JSA or an equivalent feeder grade through the relevant recruitment rules, departmental tests or a limited departmental competitive examination (LDCE), and/or seniority-based selection. There is no single India-wide eligibility rule for every UDC cadre.

For example, the Government of Puducherry’s 9 May 2023 LDCE notice proposed filling UDC vacancies from eligible Level-2 LDCs who had completed at least four years of regular service and passed its Accounts Test and Common General Departmental Tests for Ministerial Staff. A 2025 central recruitment rule instead specifies promotion from Level-2 LDCs with five years’ regular service, according to seniority-cum-fitness, with direct recruitment only if promotion is not possible. Candidates should therefore use the recruitment rules and examination notice of their own department—not a generic UDC eligibility summary—as the controlling source.
bullets: Serve in the notified feeder cadre, typically LDC/JSA or its recognised equivalent.
Meet the cadre-specific qualifying-service requirement.
Complete departmental tests or qualify in an LDCE where the rules require them.
Clear Departmental Promotion Committee, seniority-cum-fitness, vacancy and other service-record requirements where applicable.

sections.heading: Skills and knowledge
paragraphs: The work calls for reliable office-procedure and records-management capability: handling correspondence, maintaining files and service records, preparing returns and bills, and working accurately with accounts or stores documentation where allocated. These are practical job skills evidenced by the official UDC duty schedule rather than universal qualification requirements.

Computer and typing competence can also matter, but requirements vary by employer and entry mode. For example, an ICMR recruitment notice for direct UDC recruitment specified a recognised degree plus computer typing at 35 wpm in English or 30 wpm in Hindi; ISRO specifies computer proficiency for its UDC recruitment. These examples should not be treated as automatic requirements for every promotion-based UDC cadre.
bullets: File, record, correspondence and office-procedure discipline.
Accuracy with establishment records, returns, bills and accounts documentation.
Computer office applications and, where stipulated, typing in English or Hindi.
Working knowledge of the departmental rules, forms and procedures relevant to the assigned section.

sections.heading: Workplace and posting context
paragraphs: UDCs work in government offices and administrative sections, with assignments shaped by the organisation’s functions. Official examples place UDCs in administration, accounts, cash, stores and general section work; the same post may therefore have a different daily mix of duties in a technical institute, ministry, field office or autonomous body.

The post can also be subject to transfer or posting within an organisation’s administrative network. The Office of the Development Commissioner (MSME), for example, publishes promotion-cum-posting orders from LDC to UDC and from UDC to Office Superintendent, illustrating that cadre administration and location are employer-controlled.
bullets: Predominantly office-based, section-oriented administrative work.
Possible placements include administration, establishment, accounts, cash, stores, records or general sections.
Posting, workload and functional duties are determined by the employing department or organisation.

sections.heading: Progression
paragraphs: UDC can be an intermediate grade in a departmental clerical-administrative ladder, but the next post is cadre-specific. The Office of the Development Commissioner (MSME) has issued orders for promotion from UDC, Group ‘C’, Level 4 to Office Superintendent, Group ‘B’, non-gazetted, Level 6. ISRO separately states that its UDCs can progress to Assistant in Level 7, subject to vacancy availability and satisfactory performance.

Accordingly, a serving UDC should consult the current recruitment rules, seniority list, departmental promotion orders and any LDCE notice for their own organisation. These documents determine feeder eligibility, vacancies, reservation, assessment method and the actual next promotional grade.
bullets: Potential next grades may include Office Superintendent, Assistant or another cadre-specific supervisory/administrative post.
Availability of sanctioned vacancies and satisfactory service performance can affect advancement.
Do not assume that a progression path in one ministry, state government or autonomous body applies to another.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: As of the sources reviewed, a common central-government UDC benchmark is 7th CPC Pay Level 4, with a basic-pay matrix range of ₹25,500–81,100. The 2024 Ministry of Home Affairs recruitment rules and the Variable Energy Cyclotron Centre’s published designation/pay-level list both show UDC at Level 4 with that range.

This is dated pay-matrix context, not a guaranteed salary quote. Basic pay within the matrix depends on pay fixation and service history; gross and take-home pay vary with allowances, deductions, posting location, employer, state/UT rules and subsequent pay-revision decisions. Some organisations also use differently structured clerical cadres, so the relevant appointment or promotion order should be checked.
bullets: Common central benchmark: Pay Level 4, ₹25,500–81,100 basic-pay matrix.
Total monthly remuneration is variable and is not the same as the matrix’s starting basic pay.
Verify current pay level, allowances and fixation in the applicable departmental order or vacancy notice.
